entry.value:

","createdAtIso":"2025-10-29T05:26:13.321Z","lastInteractedAtIso":"2025-10-29T23:26:40.632Z","chatHistory":[{"request_id":"ab5a13af-2671-42b6-9f88-dcceacdbfe80","uuid":"9d3d0840-314b-447c-8c51-248eff002063","chatItemType":"agentic-checkpoint-delimiter","status":"success","fromTimestamp":0,"toTimestamp":1761715573322,"seen_state":"unseen"},{"chatItemType":"exchange-pointer","exchangeUuid":"61631f25-0f36-40ae-a5c6-0d226b900c3f","timestamp":"2025-10-29T05:28:52.910Z","request_message":"please provide usage instructions for this github action triggered from my fork and this branch. use all the engines except the graph engine","status":"success","seen_state":"unseen"},{"chatItemType":"exchange-pointer","exchangeUuid":"6366c7f7-2319-43eb-bd4e-a17089e2f6bf","timestamp":"2025-10-29T05:29:03.256Z","request_message":"","status":"success","seen_state":"unseen"},{"chatItemType":"exchange-pointer","exchangeUuid":"4c48a0c0-bfd4-4608-840c-e3dfe4041bf5","timestamp":"2025-10-29T05:29:07.455Z","request_message":"","status":"success","seen_state":"unseen"},{"chatItemType":"exchange-pointer","exchangeUuid":"53a6bbe4-636a-42dc-b2b6-46f4c0504fa0","timestamp":"2025-10-29T05:29:17.171Z","request_message":"","status":"success","seen_state":"unseen"},{"chatItemType":"exchange-pointer","exchangeUuid":"60372325-29dd-4fb4-a27a-153731ff74e5","timestamp":"2025-10-29T06:51:16.825Z","request_message":"the new version of sf code-analyzer relies on a config file that needs to be passed into this action. once we have that, the actual mechanism of running the code-analyzer is to call `sf code-analyzer run -c <path to config.yml> -f results.sarif` This will generate a valid sarif file, so the code in this action to do so can be deprecated. however, the action should still filter the sarif file's findings to show only the files that were modified, and only if the violation occurs within the actual changed section of the file. any questions? ","status":"success","seen_state":"unseen"},{"chatItemType":"exchange-pointer","exchangeUuid":"38bea3c6-6a11-40f3-bd74-e6c18d017de6","timestamp":"2025-10-29T06:54:17.627Z","request_message":"1. add a new input param for the config file. 
2. ditch backwards compatability. you can deprecate the engine, pmd config etc.

","status":"success","seen_state":"unseen"},{"chatItemType":"exchange-pointer","exchangeUuid":"878cac58-2475-475b-b575-8d1f9e17d4aa","timestamp":"2025-10-29T06:54:29.982Z","request_message":"","status":"success","seen_state":"unseen"},{"chatItemType":"exchange-pointer","exchangeUuid":"48f99412-4211-411e-83d9-26cb301f069b","timestamp":"2025-10-29T06:54:36.118Z","request_message":"","status":"success","seen_state":"unseen"},{"chatItemType":"exchange-pointer","exchangeUuid":"c7068dba-e393-4d42-909a-b02399731a8e","timestamp":"2025-10-29T06:55:03.629Z","request_message":"","status":"success","seen_state":"unseen"},{"chatItemType":"exchange-pointer","exchangeUuid":"3a6644c3-1a9f-401b-b880-6a4f00ca25e5","timestamp":"2025-10-29T06:55:14.865Z","request_message":"","status":"success","seen_state":"unseen"},{"chatItemType":"exchange-pointer","exchangeUuid":"7c3354ff-7703-4ea0-93ac-bdcb3dda4056","timestamp":"2025-10-29T06:55:37.104Z","request_message":"","status":"success","seen_state":"unseen"},{"chatItemType":"exchange-pointer","exchangeUuid":"dc2405f1-f23d-4ec3-900d-37b8a5e13bd2","timestamp":"2025-10-29T06:56:01.506Z","request_message":"","status":"success","seen_state":"unseen"},{"chatItemType":"exchange-pointer","exchangeUuid":"62a8ed12-2860-42c5-86a7-6d68ceb002c9","timestamp":"2025-10-29T06:56:16.734Z","request_message":"","status":"success","seen_state":"unseen"},{"chatItemType":"exchange-pointer","exchangeUuid":"e5f2c695-eb59-46a9-9edc-641dd1b96452","timestamp":"2025-10-29T06:56:40.545Z","request_message":"","status":"success","seen_state":"unseen"},{"chatItemType":"history-summary","summaryVersion":3,"request_id":"561faea0-6fb4-41be-bc0d-4a0dbf7f92ac","request_message":"

Abridged conversation history:
<abridged_history>
<user>
please provide usage instructions for this github action triggered from my fork and this branch. use all the engines except the graph engine
</user>
<agent_actions>

		.
		action.yml
		README.md
	</files_viewed>
</agent_actions>
<agent_response>
Based on my exploration of the codebase, I can now provide you with comprehensive usage instructions. The Salesforce Code Analyzer v5 supports the following engines (excluding graph engine as you requested): **pmd**, **eslint**, **eslint-lwc**, **retire-js**, and **cpd**.

Here are the usage instructions for your GitHub Action from your fork and branch:

---

# Usage Instructions: SFDX Scan Pull Request (All Engines Except Graph)

## Overview
This GitHub Action runs Salesforce Code Analyzer (v5) on pull requests and generates inline comments or check-run annotations with code quality findings.

## Prerequisites
- **Java 11+** (required for PMD engine)
- **Node.js 16+**
- **Salesforce CLI** with Code Analyzer plugin
- **GITHUB_TOKEN** with appropriate permissions

## Basic Workflow Setup

Create a workflow file in your repository at `.github/workflows/code-analysis.yml`:

```yaml
name: Salesforce Code Analysis
on:
 pull_request:
 types: [opened, reopened, synchronize]
 workflow_d...verity-threshold: 2
```

### Custom PMD Rules
```yaml
with:
 engine: 'pmd'
 pmdconfig: '.github/pmd-ruleset.xml'
 custom-pmd-rules: |
 [
 {"path": "custom-rules/apex-rules.jar", "language": "apex"},
 {"path": "custom-rules/xml-rules.xml", "language": "xml"}
 ]
```

## Troubleshooting

1. **Java version issues**: Ensure Java 11+ is installed for PMD
2. **Rate limiting**: Adjust `rate-limit-wait-time` and `rate-limit-retry-count`
3. **Too many comments**: Increase `max-number-of-comments` or use `report-mode: 'check-runs'`
4. **Debug mode**: Set `debug: 'true'` for verbose logging

## Notes

- Replace `YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME` with your GitHub username
- Replace `YOUR_BRANCH_NAME` with your branch name
- The action scans only changed files in PRs by default
- Use `target` parameter to scan entire directories
- Graph engine is intentionally excluded from these examples

---

This configuration will run all available engines except the graph engine on your pull requests!

</agent_response>

<user>
the new version of sf code-analyzer relies on a config file that needs to be passed into this action. once we have that, the actual mechanism of running the code-analyzer is to call `sf code-analyzer run -c <path to config.yml> -f results.sarif` This will generate a valid sarif file, so the code in this action to do so can be deprecated. however, the action should still filter the sarif file's findings to show only the files that were modified, and only if the violation occurs within the actual changed section of the file. any questions? 
</user>
<agent_response>
I understand the requirements. Let me clarify a few things before proceeding:

1. **Config file input**: Should I add a new input parameter (e.g., `code-analyzer-config` or `config-file`) to `action.yml` that accepts the path to the Code Analyzer v5 config YAML file?

2. **Backward compatibility**: Do you want to maintain backward compatibility with the existing inputs (`engine`, `pmdconfig`, `eslintconfig`, etc.) by generating a config file on-the-fly if the new config parameter isn't provided? Or should we deprecate those inputs entirely?

3. **SARIF output path**: Should the output file path be configurable, or can we use a fixed name like `sfdx-scan.sarif` (as currently done)?

4. **Deprecated code removal**: You mentioned the code to generate SARIF can be deprecated. Should I:
 - Remove the SARIF generation logic entirely?
 - Keep the SARIF parsing/filtering logic (since we still need to filter findings)?
 - Update the `SfCLI` class to use the new command format?

5. **Target files**: Currently the action builds a comma-separated list of files to scan (`this.scannerFlags.target = filesToScan.join(",")`). With the new approach:
 - Should we still pass specific files to scan, or let the config file determine what to scan and then filter the SARIF results?
 - Or should we write the list of changed files to a temporary file and reference it in the config?

6. **Flow scanner**: The current code has a `run-flow-scanner` option. How should this be handled with the new config-based approach?

Please clarify these points so I can implement the changes correctly!

</agent_response>

<user>
1. add a new input param for the config file. 
2. ditch backwards compatability. you can deprecate the engine, pmd config etc. 
3. let's use a configurable output name for the sarif file. default to sfca-results.sarif
4. remove teh sarif generation code. keep the parsing / filtering logic. Update classes as needed. 
5. let the config file scan everything, then filter the sarif results. 
6. the new scanner runs flow-scanner by default, so deprecate that option, defer to config file. 
</user>
<agent_actions>
	<files_modified>
		action.yml
		src/common.ts
		src/sfdxCli.ts
		src/sfdxCli.types.ts
		src/SfScannerPullRequest.ts
	</files_modified>
</agent_actions>
<agent_continues/>

</abridged_history>

Summary was generated by Agent(you) so 'I' in the summary represents Agent(you).
Here is the summary:
<summary>
1. Previous Conversation:
The user initially requested usage instructions for a GitHub Action triggered from their fork and branch, specifically wanting to use all engines except the graph engine. I provided comprehensive usage instructions for the SFDX Scan Pull Request action, detailing how to configure it with various engines (pmd, eslint, eslint-lwc, retire-js, cpd).

The user then informed me that the new version of sf code-analyzer relies on a config file approach and requested a major refactor of the action. The new approach requires:
- Passing a config file to the action
- Running the scanner with `sf code-analyzer run -c <path to config.yml> -f results.sarif`
- The scanner now generates valid SARIF files directly (deprecating internal SARIF generation code)
- The action should still filter SARIF findings to show only modified files and violations within changed sections
- Backward compatibility should be ditched entirely
- Several inputs should be deprecated (engine, pmdconfig, eslintconfig, eslint-env, tsconfig, category, run-flow-scanner, custom-pmd-rules)

2. Current Work:
I am in the process of refactoring the GitHub Action to use the new Code Analyzer v5 config-based approach. I created a task list with 8 tasks and have completed 4 of them:

**Completed:**
- Updated action.yml inputs (added code-analyzer-config as required, sarif-output-file with default "sfca-results.sarif", removed deprecated inputs)
- Updated ScannerFlags type to only include configFile and outfile
- Refactored SfCLI class to use new command format `sf code-analyzer run -c <config> -f <output>`
- Updated PluginInputs type to remove deprecated fields (customPmdRules, target, runFlowScanner)

**In Progress:**
- Currently updating SfScannerPullRequest class - just modified the constructor to use new inputs
- Need to update validateContext method to remove target parameter
- Need to update workflow method to scan all files (not just changed files) then filter SARIF results

3. Key Technical Concepts:
- Salesforce Code Analyzer v5 with config file approach
- SARIF (Static Analysis Results Interchange Format) file parsing and filtering
- GitHub Actions with TypeScript/Node.js
- Pull request diff analysis to identify changed files and line numbers
- Filtering violations to only show those in modified code sections
- GitHub API integration for comments and check-runs reporting
- Deprecation of multiple input parameters in favor of config file approach

4. Relevant Files and Code:

- **action.yml**
 - Main GitHub Action configuration file
 - Changed from multiple engine-specific inputs to single config file input
 - New inputs: `code-analyzer-config` (required), `sarif-output-file` (default: "sfca-results.sarif")
 - Removed inputs: category, engine, eslint-env, eslintconfig, pmdconfig, custom-pmd-rules, target, tsconfig, run-flow-scanner
 - Kept inputs: severity-threshold, strictly-enforced-rules, report-mode, delete-resolved-comments, max-number-of-comments, rate-limit-wait-time, rate-limit-retry-count, comment-batch-size, debug, export-sarif

- **src/sfdxCli.types.ts**
 - Simplified ScannerFlags type:
 ```typescript
 export type ScannerFlags = {
 configFile: string;
 outfile: string;
 };
 ```

- **src/sfdxCli.ts**
 - Refactored to use new command format
 - Removed `generateSarifOutputFile()` and `registerRule()` methods
 - Added new `runCodeAnalyzer()` method:
 ```typescript
 private async runCodeAnalyzer() {
 try {
 console.log("Executing sf code-analyzer with config file...");
 const cliCommand = `sf code-analyzer run -c "${this.scannerFlags.configFile}" -f "${this.scannerFlags.outfile}"`;
 console.log(`Running command: ${cliCommand}`);
 execSync(cliCommand, {
 maxBuffer: 10485760,
 stdio: 'inherit'
 });
 console.log("Code analyzer execution completed successfully");
 } catch (err) {
 console.error("Error running code analyzer:", err);
 throw err;
 }
 }
 ```
 - Extracted SARIF parsing into separate `parseSarifFile()` method
 - Kept all SARIF parsing/filtering logic intact

- **src/common.ts**
 - Updated PluginInputs type to remove: customPmdRules, target, runFlowScanner
 - Kept: severityThreshold, strictlyEnforcedRules, maxNumberOfComments, commentBatchSize, rateLimitWaitTime, rateLimitRetries, deleteResolvedComments, reportMode, debug, exportSarif

- **src/SfScannerPullRequest.ts**
 - Updated constructor to use new inputs:
 ```typescript
 constructor() {
 const configFile = getInput("code-analyzer-config");
 if (!configFile) {
 setFailed("code-analyzer-config input is required");
 throw new Error("code-analyzer-config input is required");
 }

 this.scannerFlags = {
 configFile: configFile,
 outfile: getInput("sarif-output-file") || "sfca-results.sarif",
 };

 this.inputs = {
 reportMode: getInput("report-mode") || "check-runs",
 maxNumberOfComments: parseInt(getInput("max-number-of-comments")) || 100,
 rateLimitWaitTime: parseInt(getInput("rate-limit-wait-time")) || 60000,
 rateLimitRetries: parseInt(getInput("rate-limit-retries")) || 5,
 commentBatchSize: parseInt(getInput("comment-batch-size")) || 15,
 severityThreshold: this.validateThresholdInput(),
 strictlyEnforcedRules: getInput("strictly-enforced-rules"),
 deleteResolvedComments: getInput("delete-resolved-comments") === "true",
 debug: getInput("debug") === "true",
 exportSarif: getInput("export-sarif") === "true",
 };
 ```
 - Changed validateContext call to remove target parameter
 - Still need to update: validateContext method signature, workflow method, getFilesToScan method, registerCustomScannerRules method removal

5. Problem Solving:
- Successfully removed all deprecated input handling from constructor
- Simplified ScannerFlags to only contain config file path and output file path
- Maintained SARIF parsing logic while removing SARIF generation logic
- The new approach lets the config file determine what to scan, then filters results to only show violations in changed lines of modified files

6. Pending Tasks and Next Steps:

**Remaining tasks from task list:**
- Update SfScannerPullRequest class (IN_PROGRESS) - need to complete:
 - Update validateContext method to remove target parameter
 - Update workflow method to scan all files (via config) then filter SARIF results
 - Remove getFilesToScan method (no longer needed since config determines what to scan)
 - Remove registerCustomScannerRules method (deprecated with custom-pmd-rules input)
 - Update performStaticCodeAnalysisOnFilesInDiff method name/logic
 - Update filterFindingsToDiffScope to not check inputs.target
- Update tests (NOT_STARTED)
- Update README documentation (NOT_STARTED)

**Immediate next steps:**
1. Update validateContext method signature from `validateContext(pullRequest: GithubPullRequest, target: string)` to `validateContext(pullRequest: GithubPullRequest)`
2. Update the workflow method to remove file scanning logic and custom rule registration - the new flow should be: get diff -> run scanner on everything (config determines scope) -> filter SARIF results to changed lines
3. Remove or refactor getFilesToScan method since we no longer pass specific files to the scanner
4. Remove registerCustomScannerRules method entirely
5. Update filterFindingsToDiffScope to remove the `!this.inputs.target` check since target no longer exists

</summary>

Continue the conversation and finish the task given by the user from this point.
</supervisor>","response_text":"Ok. I will continue the conversation from this point.","structured_output_nodes":[{"id":2,"type":0,"content":"Ok. I will continue the conversation from this point."},{"id":1,"type":5,"content":"","tool_use":{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_012wRcZLUpJQMPBC2ffVJWfU","tool_name":"str-replace-editor","input_json":"{"command": "str_replace", "path": "src/SfScannerPullRequest.ts", "instruction_reminder": "ALWAYS BREAK DOWN EDITS INTO SMALLER CHUNKS OF AT MOST 150 LINES EACH.", "old_str_1": " /**\n * @desscription validates that the execution context is a pull request, and that we have a valid target reference\n * @param pullRequest\n * @param target\n */\n private validateContext(pullRequest: GithubPullRequest, target: string) {\n console.log(\n \"Validating that this action was invoked from an acceptable context...\"\n );\n if (!pullRequest && !target) {\n setFailed(\n \"This action is only applicable when invoked by a pull request, or with the target property supplied.\"\n );\n }\n }", "old_str_start_line_number_1": 100, "old_str_end_line_number_1": 114, "new_str_1": " /**\n * @description validates that the execution context is a pull request\n * @param pullRequest\n */\n private validateContext(pullRequest: GithubPullRequest) {\n console.log(\n \"Validating that this action was invoked from an acceptable context...\"\n );\n if (!pullRequest) {\n setFailed(\n \"This action is only applicable when invoked by a pull request.\"\n );\n }\n }"}","mcp_server_name":"","mcp_tool_name":""}}],"status":"success","seen_state":"seen","timestamp":"2025-10-29T07:06:57.112Z"},{"chatItemType":"history-summary","summaryVersion":3,"request_id":"395e5b21-9cc2-49be-8e0f-639cc24b6392","request_message":"

<agent_response>
Based on my exploration of the codebase, I can now provide you with comprehensive usage instructions. The Salesforce Code Analyzer v5 supports the following engines (excluding graph engine as you requested): **pmd**, **eslint**, **eslint-lwc**, **retire-js**, and **cpd**.

Here are the usage instructions for your GitHub Action from your fork and branch:

---

# Usage Instructions: SFDX Scan Pull Request (All Engines Except Graph)

## Overview
This GitHub Action runs Salesforce Code Analyzer (v5) on pull requests and generates inline comments or check-run annotations with code quality findings.

## Prerequisites
- **Java 11+** (required for PMD engine)
- **Node.js 16+**
- **Salesforce CLI** with Code Analyzer plugin
- **GITHUB_TOKEN** with appropriate permissions

## Basic Workflow Setup

Create a workflow file in your repository at `.github/workflows/code-analysis.yml`:

```yaml
name: Salesforce Code Analysis
on:
 pull_request:
 types: [opened, reopened, synchronize]
 workflow_d...verity-threshold: 2
```

### Custom PMD Rules
```yaml
with:
 engine: 'pmd'
 pmdconfig: '.github/pmd-ruleset.xml'
 custom-pmd-rules: |
 [
 {"path": "custom-rules/apex-rules.jar", "language": "apex"},
 {"path": "custom-rules/xml-rules.xml", "language": "xml"}
 ]
```

## Troubleshooting

1. **Java version issues**: Ensure Java 11+ is installed for PMD
2. **Rate limiting**: Adjust `rate-limit-wait-time` and `rate-limit-retry-count`
3. **Too many comments**: Increase `max-number-of-comments` or use `report-mode: 'check-runs'`
4. **Debug mode**: Set `debug: 'true'` for verbose logging

## Notes

- Replace `YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME` with your GitHub username
- Replace `YOUR_BRANCH_NAME` with your branch name
- The action scans only changed files in PRs by default
- Use `target` parameter to scan entire directories
- Graph engine is intentionally excluded from these examples

---

This configuration will run all available engines except the graph engine on your pull requests!

</agent_response>

<user>
the new version of sf code-analyzer relies on a config file that needs to be passed into this action. once we have that, the actual mechanism of running the code-analyzer is to call `sf code-analyzer run -c <path to config.yml> -f results.sarif` This will generate a valid sarif file, so the code in this action to do so can be deprecated. however, the action should still filter the sarif file's findings to show only the files that were modified, and only if the violation occurs within the actual changed section of the file. any questions? 
</user>
<agent_response>
I understand the requirements. Let me clarify a few things before proceeding:

1. **Config file input**: Should I add a new input parameter (e.g., `code-analyzer-config` or `config-file`) to `action.yml` that accepts the path to the Code Analyzer v5 config YAML file?

2. **Backward compatibility**: Do you want to maintain backward compatibility with the existing inputs (`engine`, `pmdconfig`, `eslintconfig`, etc.) by generating a config file on-the-fly if the new config parameter isn't provided? Or should we deprecate those inputs entirely?

3. **SARIF output path**: Should the output file path be configurable, or can we use a fixed name like `sfdx-scan.sarif` (as currently done)?

4. **Deprecated code removal**: You mentioned the code to generate SARIF can be deprecated. Should I:
 - Remove the SARIF generation logic entirely?
 - Keep the SARIF parsing/filtering logic (since we still need to filter findings)?
 - Update the `SfCLI` class to use the new command format?

5. **Target files**: Currently the action builds a comma-separated list of files to scan (`this.scannerFlags.target = filesToScan.join(",")`). With the new approach:
 - Should we still pass specific files to scan, or let the config file determine what to scan and then filter the SARIF results?
 - Or should we write the list of changed files to a temporary file and reference it in the config?

6. **Flow scanner**: The current code has a `run-flow-scanner` option. How should this be handled with the new config-based approach?

Please clarify these points so I can implement the changes correctly!

</agent_response>

<user>
1. add a new input param for the config file. 
2. ditch backwards compatability. you can deprecate the engine, pmd config etc. 
3. let's use a configurable output name for the sarif file. default to sfca-results.sarif
4. remove teh sarif generation code. keep the parsing / filtering logic. Update classes as needed. 
5. let the config file scan everything, then filter the sarif results. 
6. the new scanner runs flow-scanner by default, so deprecate that option, defer to config file. 
</user>
<agent_actions>
	<files_modified>
		action.yml
		src/common.ts
		src/sfdxCli.ts
		src/sfdxCli.types.ts
		src/SfScannerPullRequest.ts
	</files_modified>
</agent_actions>

Summary was generated by Agent(you) so 'I' in the summary represents Agent(you).
Here is the summary:
<summary>
1. Previous Conversation:
The user initially requested usage instructions for a GitHub Action (sfdx-scan-pull-request) triggered from their fork and branch, specifically wanting to use all engines except the graph engine. I provided comprehensive usage instructions covering all available Salesforce Code Analyzer engines (pmd, eslint, eslint-lwc, retire-js, cpd).

The user then informed me that the new version of sf code-analyzer relies on a config file approach and requested a major refactoring of the action. The new approach requires:
- Passing a config file to the action
- Running the scanner with `sf code-analyzer run -c <path to config.yml> -f results.sarif`
- The scanner now generates valid SARIF files directly (deprecating internal SARIF generation code)
- The action should still filter SARIF findings to show only modified files and only violations within changed sections
- Backward compatibility should be ditched entirely
- Multiple deprecated inputs should be removed

2. Current Work:
I am currently refactoring the GitHub Action to use the new Code Analyzer v5 config-based approach. I created a task list with 8 tasks and have been working through them systematically:

**Completed Tasks:**
- ✅ Updated action.yml inputs - Added `code-analyzer-config` (required) and `sarif-output-file` (default: "sfca-results.sarif"), removed deprecated inputs (engine, pmdconfig, eslintconfig, eslint-env, tsconfig, category, run-flow-scanner, custom-pmd-rules)
- ✅ Updated ScannerFlags type - Simplified to only include `configFile` and `outfile`
- ✅ Updated SfCLI class - Replaced old SARIF generation logic with new `runCodeAnalyzer()` method that uses `sf code-analyzer run -c <config> -f <output>`, kept SARIF parsing logic in `parseSarifFile()` method
- ✅ Updated PluginInputs type - Removed deprecated fields (customPmdRules, target, runFlowScanner)
- ✅ Partially updated SfScannerPullRequest constructor - Updated to use new inputs, removed deprecated input handling

**In Progress:**
- Finishing SfScannerPullRequest class updates - Need to update validateContext method, workflow method, and remove custom rule registration logic

**Remaining Tasks:**
- Update tests
- Update README documentation

3. Key Technical Concepts:
- **Salesforce Code Analyzer v5**: New version uses config file approach instead of command-line flags
- **SARIF (Static Analysis Results Interchange Format)**: Standard format for static analysis results, now generated directly by the scanner
- **GitHub Actions**: Custom action for running code analysis on pull requests
- **Diff-based filtering**: Action filters SARIF results to only show violations in changed files and changed lines
- **TypeScript**: Action is written in TypeScript and compiled to JavaScript
- **Node.js 20**: Runtime environment for the action
- **Deprecated inputs**: engine, pmdconfig, eslintconfig, eslint-env, tsconfig, category, run-flow-scanner, custom-pmd-rules, target
- **New inputs**: code-analyzer-config (required), sarif-output-file (optional, default: "sfca-results.sarif")
- **Retained inputs**: severity-threshold, strictly-enforced-rules, report-mode, delete-resolved-comments, max-number-of-comments, rate-limit-wait-time, rate-limit-retry-count, comment-batch-size, debug, export-sarif

4. Relevant Files and Code:

- **action.yml**
 - Main GitHub Action configuration file
 - Updated to use new config-based approach
 - Removed 8 deprecated inputs, added 2 new inputs
 ```yaml
 inputs:
 code-analyzer-config:
 description: "Path to the Code Analyzer configuration YAML file (required)"
 required: true
 sarif-output-file:
 description: "Path where the SARIF output file will be generated"
 default: "sfca-results.sarif"
 ```

- **src/sfdxCli.types.ts**
 - Type definitions for scanner flags
 - Simplified ScannerFlags from 11 fields to 2 fields
 ```typescript
 export type ScannerFlags = {
 configFile: string;
 outfile: string;
 };
 ```

- **src/sfdxCli.ts**
 - Core CLI interface class
 - Removed `generateSarifOutputFile()` and `registerRule()` methods
 - Added new `runCodeAnalyzer()` method that executes: `sf code-analyzer run -c "${this.scannerFlags.configFile}" -f "${this.scannerFlags.outfile}"`
 - Refactored `getFindingsForFiles()` to call `runCodeAnalyzer()` then `parseSarifFile()`
 - Kept SARIF parsing logic in new `parseSarifFile()` method
 ```typescript
 private async runCodeAnalyzer() {
 try {
 console.log("Executing sf code-analyzer with config file...");
 const cliCommand = `sf code-analyzer run -c "${this.scannerFlags.configFile}" -f "${this.scannerFlags.outfile}"`;
 console.log(`Running command: ${cliCommand}`);
 execSync(cliCommand, {
 maxBuffer: 10485760,
 stdio: 'inherit'
 });
 console.log("Code analyzer execution completed successfully");
 } catch (err) {
 console.error("Error running code analyzer:", err);
 throw err;
 }
 }
 ```

- **src/common.ts**
 - Common types and utilities
 - Updated PluginInputs type to remove deprecated fields
 ```typescript
 export type PluginInputs = {
 severityThreshold: number;
 strictlyEnforcedRules: string;
 maxNumberOfComments: number;
 commentBatchSize: number;
 rateLimitWaitTime: number;
 rateLimitRetries: number;
 deleteResolvedComments: boolean;
 reportMode: string | "comments" | "check-runs";
 debug: boolean;
 exportSarif: boolean;
 };
 ```

- **src/SfScannerPullRequest.ts**
 - Main workflow orchestration class
 - Updated constructor to use new inputs (configFile, outfile)
 - Added validation for required code-analyzer-config input
 - Removed references to deprecated inputs (category, engine, env, eslintconfig, pmdconfig, tsConfig, customPmdRules, target, runFlowScanner)
 - Still needs updates to: validateContext method (remove target parameter), workflow method (remove file filtering before scan, remove custom rule registration), and removal of getFilesToScan and registerCustomScannerRules methods

- **README.md**
 - User currently has this file open
 - Needs to be updated with new usage instructions reflecting config-based approach

5. Problem Solving:
No major problems encountered yet. The refactoring is proceeding systematically by:
1. Updating type definitions first
2. Updating core CLI logic
3. Updating main workflow class
4. Will update tests and documentation last

The approach maintains the core filtering logic (only show violations in changed files/lines) while simplifying the scanner invocation to use the new config file approach.

6. Pending Tasks and Next Steps:

**Current Task - Update SfScannerPullRequest class (IN_PROGRESS):**
Need to complete the following changes to src/SfScannerPullRequest.ts:

1. Update `validateContext` method signature - Remove the `target` parameter since it's deprecated:
 ```typescript
 private validateContext(pullRequest: GithubPullRequest) {
 console.log("Validating that this action was invoked from an acceptable context...");
 if (!pullRequest) {
 setFailed("This action is only applicable when invoked by a pull request.");
 }
 }
 ```

2. Update `workflow` method - Remove file filtering before scan (let config file determine what to scan), remove custom rule registration, simplify to scan everything then filter SARIF results:
 - Remove `getFilesToScan()` call
 - Remove `this.scannerFlags.target = filesToScan.join(",")` line
 - Remove `registerCustomScannerRules()` call and conditional
 - Keep the diff retrieval for filtering purposes
 - Keep the SARIF filtering logic

3. Remove deprecated methods:
 - Remove `getFilesToScan()` method (lines 204-218)
 - Remove `registerCustomScannerRules()` method (lines 224-241)

**Next Tasks:**
- Update tests (tests/sfdx-cli.test.ts) to use new config-based approach
- Update README.md with new usage instructions showing how to create and use a Code Analyzer config file
- Build and test the changes

**Exact quote from most recent work:**
I was in the middle of updating the SfScannerPullRequest constructor and had just completed this change:
```typescript
this.pullRequest = context?.payload?.pull_request;
this.validateContext(this.pullRequest);
```
The next step is to update the `validateContext` method definition to match this new signature (removing the `target` parameter), then update the `workflow` method to remove file-based targeting and custom rule registration.

</summary>

Continue the conversation and finish the task given by the user from this point.

context is a pull request, and that we have a valid target reference\n * @param pullRequest\n * @param target\n */\n private validateContext(pullRequest: GithubPullRequest, target: string) {\n console.log(\n \"Validating that this action was invoked from an acceptable context...\"\n );\n if (!pullRequest && !target) {\n setFailed(\n \"This action is only applicable when invoked by a pull
